labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

labels.name: wontfix
labels.description: This will not be worked on

issues.title: Make it clear who has authority for the final check
issues.author: rgwilton
issues.body:
IANA/RFC Editor runs the tools to check that the version of the doc to be published has the expected version.

If the tools report that it should be a major version but the approved version was a minor change, then as part of the AUTH48 process then the authors/ADs MUST sign off on releasing as a major version change.  YANG Doctors may be consulted if necessary to ensure that the right version is selected.

comments.author: rgwilton
comments.body:
From the meeting today:
 
We’ll have RFC editor and/or IANA folks run pyang with --check-update-semver.  If the suggested SemVer doesn’t match the one in the draft, then punt back to the AD to sort it out.  (I likely don’t have this quite correct, but it was something along those lines).
 
We’ll stick with pyang suggesting a minor bump when there are purely “questionable” changes (description, must, when, pattern). Maybe a note for Shepard’s checklist that all warnings have to have been chased down with authors?
 
Proposal for the IANA SemVer Guidelines doc: get it to a “usable draft”, and then let IANA actually use it for a few months (on at least a handful of actual updated YANG modules) before we bring it to WG LC.

issues.title: Add better tool examples (including output from Joe).
issues.author: rgwilton

pulls.title: Address some nits and add comments.
pulls.author: xorrkaz
pulls.body: I fixed a few formatting and spelling nits.  I left some comments with `[JMC:]`, and I changed the example version from 2.5.0 to 2.1.0 to avoid any IANA confusion stemming from skipping versions.
